title: Derris campestris
taxonomy: Derris campestris is a plant species classified as a member of the Fabaceae family. This family is commonly known as the legume family. Derris campestris belongs to the genus Derris and is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It is a climbing shrub that is often used for its insecticidal properties.
uses: Derris campestris is a plant species traditionally used in folk medicine. The extracts of Derris campestris have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots and leaves of Derris campestris are often used in traditional medicine, and they are believed to have analgesic and anti-arthritis effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Derris campestris for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Derris campestris, also known as the wild derris or kudriavka, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant contains rotenone, a toxic compound that has been traditionally used as an insecticide and has also been researched for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Some studies suggest that Derris campestris may have anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm this. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could make it useful for treating certain infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential risks of using Derris campestris.
properties: Derris campestris is known for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic activities. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit potential antimicrobial properties, making it a promising candidate for the development of new medicines. Additionally, Derris campestris has been reported to possess antiparasitic and antiviral properties, which can help combat certain diseases. Further research is needed to fully explore the therapeutic potential of this plant.
constituents: Derris campestris, a plant species, contains active constituents such as rotenone and derrisone, which are toxic to insects and have been used as a natural insecticide. The plant's active compounds are responsible for its insecticidal properties and have been used for pest control. The rotenone in Derris campestris is a potent neurotoxin that affects the nervous system of insects, ultimately leading to their death. The plant's active constituents have also been used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world. However, the use of Derris campestris as an insecticide has largely been phased out due to concerns over its toxicity to humans and other non-target organisms.
parts: Derris campestris primarily contains rotenone in its roots and other underground parts, which are used for their medicinal properties. The roots have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. They are also said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots are a rich source of rotenone, which has been used in modern medicine to treat certain types of cancer and as an insecticide. The leaves and stem of Derris campestris are also used in some traditional remedies, although to a lesser extent.
preparations: Derris campestris is often used in traditional herbal medicine due to its insecticidal and antihelmintic properties. The plant's root is typically harvested and dried for use in various herbal preparations. In traditional medicine, Derris campestris is believed to have anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Derris campestris are often used to treat worm infestations, fever, and skin infections. The plant's extracts are also used in some modern medicine formulations due to their bioactive compounds.
side_effects: Derris campestris contains a toxic compound that can cause adverse effects if ingested or inhaled in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Overuse of Derris campestris can also cause damage to the liver and kidneys. In severe cases, exposure to its toxic compounds can be fatal. It's essential to handle and use Derris campestris with caution and follow proper safety protocols.
intro: Derris campestris, also known as the wild mimosa or prairie mimosa, is a perennial legume native to tropical Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, making it effective in treating fever, wounds, and other skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Derris campestris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Derris campestris is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, fever, and wounds.
